title: Battlefield Transparency Is Rewriting the Rules of War

source.original: General Valerii Zaluzhnyi on the Impossibility of Operational-Level Tasks Due to Technological Progress — Thread by Giorgi Revishvili

body_html:
The proliferation of drones, satellite imagery, signals intelligence, and networked sensors has created a condition that military theorists feared but never fully experienced at scale: near-total battlefield transparency. In Ukraine, this condition is not hypothetical — it is the lived reality that has shaped every tactical and operational decision since 2022.

Transparency at this level destroys the preconditions for classical offensive operations. Surprise — long considered the most powerful force multiplier — becomes extraordinarily difficult to achieve when the enemy can observe troop concentrations, logistics buildup, and movement corridors in near-real time. Forces that attempt to mass for a breakthrough are detected and struck before they can execute.

The consequences are visible in the character of the fighting. Rather than fluid frontlines and rapid advances, the war in Ukraine has produced static, heavily fortified positions, grinding attritional combat, and incremental territorial changes measured in hundreds of meters. This is not because commanders lack skill or will — it is because the technological environment punishes the offense with devastating efficiency.

Ubiquitous ISR doesn't just affect the moment of attack. It reshapes the entire operational cycle: assembly areas are targeted, supply routes are interdicted, and reserves moving forward are struck en route. The cumulative effect is that the traditional operational sequence — concentrate, breach, exploit, pursue — collapses under the weight of persistent observation and precision fires.

This shift has implications far beyond Ukraine. Any military planning for a future conventional conflict must reckon with the reality that the electromagnetic and visual spectrum are now contested domains where concealment is the exception, not the norm. The age of hiding an army has effectively ended.
